papers.title_en: WestWorld: A Knowledge-Encoded Scalable Trajectory World Model for Diverse Robotic Systems
papers.authors: Yuchen Wang, Jiangtao Kong, Sizhe Wei, Xiaochang Li, Haohong Lin, Hongjue Zhao, Tianyi Zhou, Lu Gan, Huajie Shao
papers.evidence: 适用于多种机器人系统的轨迹世界模型
papers.tldr: WestWorld是一个为多样化机器人系统设计的知识编码可扩展轨迹世界模型。针对现有模型在处理多种动力学系统时扩展性差且忽视物理结构知识的问题，该研究提出了系统感知专家混合架构（Sys-MoE）和结构嵌入技术。通过在89个复杂环境（含仿真与现实）中预训练，WestWorld在零样本和少样本轨迹预测及下游控制任务中表现优异，并成功部署于真实四足机器人。
papers.abstract_en: Trajectory world models play a crucial role in robotic dynamics learning, planning, and control. While recent works have explored trajectory world models for diverse robotic systems, they struggle to scale to a large number of distinct system dynamics and overlook domain knowledge of physical structures. To address these limitations, we introduce WestWorld, a knoWledge-Encoded Scalable Trajectory World model for diverse robotic systems. To tackle the scalability challenge, we propose a novel system-aware Mixture-of-Experts (Sys-MoE) that dynamically combines and routes specialized experts for different robotic systems via a learnable system embedding. To further enhance zero-shot generalization, we incorporate domain knowledge of robot physical structures by introducing a structural embedding that aligns trajectory representations with morphological information. After pretraining on 89 complex environments spanning diverse morphologies across both simulation and real-world settings, WestWorld achieves significant improvements over competitive baselines in zero- and few-shot trajectory prediction. Additionally, it shows strong scalability across a wide range of robotic environments and significantly improves performance on downstream model-based control for different robots. Finally, we deploy our model on a real-world Unitree Go1, where it demonstrates stable locomotion performance (see our demo on the website: https://westworldrobot.github.io/). The code will be available upon publication.

papers.title_en: Full Stack Navigation, Mapping, and Planning for the Lunar Autonomy Challenge
papers.authors: Adam Dai, Asta Wu, Keidai Iiyama, Guillem Casadesus Vila, Kaila Coimbra, Thomas Deng, Grace Gao
papers.evidence: GNSS受限环境下的全栈导航与建图
papers.tldr: 本研究针对月球自主挑战赛，开发了一套适用于无GNSS、视觉挑战环境的模块化全栈自主导航与建图系统。该系统集成了语义分割、立体视觉里程计、带回环检测的位姿图SLAM以及分层规划控制。通过轻量化学习模型实现实时感知，并利用因子图后端维持全局一致性。该方案在模拟器中表现优异，实现了厘米级定位精度，并最终获得竞赛冠军。
papers.abstract_en: We present a modular, full-stack autonomy system for lunar surface navigation and mapping developed for the Lunar Autonomy Challenge. Operating in a GNSS-denied, visually challenging environment, our pipeline integrates semantic segmentation, stereo visual odometry, pose graph SLAM with loop closures, and layered planning and control. We leverage lightweight learning-based perception models for real-time segmentation and feature tracking and use a factor-graph backend to maintain globally consistent localization. High-level waypoint planning is designed to promote mapping coverage while encouraging frequent loop closures, and local motion planning uses arc sampling with geometric obstacle checks for efficient, reactive control. We evaluate our approach in the competition's high-fidelity lunar simulator, demonstrating centimeter-level localization accuracy, high-fidelity map generation, and strong repeatability across random seeds and rock distributions. Our solution achieved first place in the final competition evaluation.
